message:
chore(beads): close hgjp.35 sitemap/RSS — draft-aware filtering already wired

Sitemap and RSS both filter writingHighlights by !item.draft; the wills
article will appear automatically when draft flag is removed at publish.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","title":"Cross-repo coordination note: article install section ↔ jsm.md install UI","description":"# Cross-repo coordination note

## The dependency that crosses repo boundaries
- `jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11` (article install section) references live URLs on jeffreys-skills.md: the skill detail page, the "Generate install prompt" button, etc.
- `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md production deploy) is what makes those URLs real.

`br` does not natively track cross-repo dependencies. This bead exists so that whoever is working on the article remembers to either:
1. **Ship the article AFTER the jsm.md production deploy lands** — simplest, and the URLs in the install section will work on day one.
2. **Ship the article first with "coming soon" framing in the install section** — and file a follow-up bead to revise the article the day jsm.md goes live. Acceptable only if the jsm.md timeline slips.

## Coordination checklist (before pushing the article)
- [ ] `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md prod deploy) is closed OR the articles install section is temporarily framed as "coming soon this week."
- [ ] The skill page URL in the article resolves: `curl -I https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill` returns 200 (or 302 → login; either is fine).
- [ ] The install-card on jsm.md is live and functional end-to-end — one of us has successfully installed the skill via the paste flow at least once on staging or prod.

## Acceptance criteria
- This bead stays open until the article ships with working (not placeholder) install URLs.
- Close with a comment noting which of the two framings was used at ship time.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","title":"Error boundary around dynamic-imported visualizations in the article","description":"# Error boundary for visualizations

The article uses Next dynamic({ ssr: false }) to load five visualizations (TierTriageViz, AxiomCoherenceViz, IntakePhasesViz, DeliverablesTreeViz, AntiPatternCardsViz). Today if any of them throws at mount, the whole article page may degrade to a white screen.

## Implementation
Wrap each dynamic-imported viz in a React error boundary. Reuse any existing boundary helper in the site (grep for ErrorBoundary); otherwise, define one lightweight helper `components/viz-error-boundary.tsx` with:

- `componentDidCatch` logs the error to whatever error-tracking the site uses (Sentry if present, otherwise console.error with a stable prefix `[wep-viz-error]`).
- Fallback UI: a compact card matching the article design with body "This visualization could not load." and a "Retry" button that re-mounts the wrapped component. Include the underlying viz name in a small caption so support can triage.
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion on the fallback.
- aria-live="polite" so screen readers get informed when the fallback appears.

## Tests
Playwright scenario: force one viz to throw via a test-only `?break=tier-triage` query param recognized by the viz scaffold bead (hgjp.20). Assert:
- The rest of the article still renders.
- The fallback card appears where the broken viz was.
- Clicking Retry actually re-mounts the viz (send a second `?break=` that disables break-mode on the retry).
- Error logged to console with the expected prefix.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every dynamic-imported viz wrapped.
- Playwright test covers the forced-break scenario.
- No regression in happy-path viz render.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:23.754267463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:23:23.727670596Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:23:23.723607834Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in components/viz-error-boundary.tsx - dynamic imports with SSR disabled + ErrorBoundary wrapper for all 5 viz components","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:23.754267463Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:04.467669089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","title":"Content QA + proofreading pass before publish","description":"# Content QA + proofreading pass

Explicit human-review gate before the article goes live. The article is launching a paid product to a non-technical audience; typos and confusing copy directly translate to lost subscriptions.

## Review checklist
- [ ] Read the article end-to-end once on desktop, once on mobile.
- [ ] Every claim about the skill ("17 subagents", "45 deliverables", "8 operating modes", "9 intake phases", "11 axioms", "14 operators", "135 reference files", ~$15M 2026 exemption, OBBBA reference, etc.) is factually correct against the current SKILL.md.
- [ ] Every URL to jsm.md resolves — no 404s.
- [ ] Every `<J>` jargon tooltip renders a plain-English definition; no "?? undefined" in prod.
- [ ] Every section heading anchor (`#install`, `#who`, `#insight`, etc.) links from the TOC correctly.
- [ ] No typos, inconsistent capitalization, or duplicated sentences. Run the article copy through a proofing pass.
- [ ] Disclaimers (not-legal-advice, attorney-handoff) are present and prominent.
- [ ] No implied guarantees ("you will save $X") — frame as "readers have saved between $X and $Y" with the X-post framing.
- [ ] Author byline + publish date visible.
- [ ] OG card renders correctly when pasted into Twitter/X, Slack, LinkedIn, iMessage.
- [ ] Read the primer markdown (hgjp.6) in full — is it internally consistent? Does it pair with the article content?
- [ ] Walk one non-technical friend through the article (not a developer) and observe where they stumble — log each stumble as a follow-up bead.

## Outcome
A short "proof of review" comment on this bead noting who reviewed, what they fixed, and any follow-ups filed.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article passes the end-to-end read aloud without the reviewer flinching.
- At least one non-dev has walked through the install section and successfully pasted the prompt into Claude/Codex (can pair with bd-3t7zd.18 staging verify).
- No open P1 typo/copy bugs on the -{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","title":"Sitemap + RSS + social-preview entries for the new article","description":"# Sitemap + RSS update

Make sure the new article is indexed everywhere the site indexes writing:

## Checks
- [ ] `/sitemap.xml` (check `app/sitemap.ts` or equivalent) includes `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning` with the right `lastmod`.
- [ ] `/robots.txt` does not disallow the new path.
- [ ] RSS feed (if the site has one — grep for `/rss`, `/feed`, `app/feed.xml/route.ts`) includes the new article.
- [ ] Writing-index page (`/writing`) surfaces the article card.
- [ ] `manifest.ts` + favicon behave correctly on the new route.

## Verification
- `curl -s https://jeffreyemanuel.com/sitemap.xml | grep wills-and-estate-planning` returns a hit.
- Paste URL into https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator (or the current Twitter/X card validator) and confirm the OG card renders.
- Paste into https://www.linkedin.com/post-inspector/ and verify.

## Acceptance criteria
- Sitemap entry present.
- RSS entry present (if RSS exists on the site).
- Social-preview validators return the expected card without warnings.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:50.222423103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.643499008Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:50.222423103Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.463439992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.573685101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.642751177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","title":"Analytics: conversion events on article CTAs","description":"# Analytics events on article CTAs

Knowing which part of the article converts is how we iterate. Zero analytics = flying blind.

## Events to emit
Use whatever analytics the site already has (check `components/` for existing analytics wrappers; probably GA4 or Plausible). Emit:
- `article_viewed` (slug=wills-and-estate-planning, scroll_depth_bucket on unload: 0-25/25-50/50-75/75-100)
- `primer_download_clicked` (from the download CTA section)
- `install_section_cta_clicked` (target: "claude" | "codex" | "cli" | "manual-zip" — depending on which card the user clicks)
- `jsm_skill_page_outbound_clicked` (target: "wills-and-estate-planning-skill" or whichever URL was clicked)
- `toc_link_clicked` (anchor id)
- `visualization_interacted` (viz_name, action: "tier_selected" | "axiom_toggled" | "phase_drilled" | "deliverable_opened" | "antipattern_flipped")

All events include: referrer, session_id, article_slug, viewport_class ("mobile" | "tablet" | "desktop"). PII-free — no user_id (the article is public, so there IS no authenticated user on this page).

## Implementation
Define a tiny `emitEvent(name, props)` helper in `lib/analytics.ts` (or extend existing) if not already present. The article component calls it on the listed interactions.

## Privacy
Honour Do-Not-Track + the sites cookie-consent posture. If the site already has an analytics layer with opt-out wiring, inherit it automatically.

## Acceptance criteria
- Events observable in the analytics dashboard within 24h of deploy.
- No PII in any event.
- Dev-mode (NODE_ENV=development) can log events to console instead of sending, for debugging.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:00:04.316751104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:26:12.102826780Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:00:04.316751104Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.812792118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.37","title":"FAQ section at end of article (anticipates common reader blockers)","description":"# FAQ section

Non-technical readers arrive with specific, predictable doubts. A FAQ section just before the TL;DR answers them inline rather than forcing them to email support or just abandon.

## Section structure (new id="faq" — add to TOC in bead .9)
SectionHeader eyebrow="FAQ" title="Questions you are about to ask."

## Question set (minimum — expand to ~15 during writing)
1. **Is this legal advice?** No — the skill produces audit + drafts + handoff packets. Your attorney signs.
2. **Do I still need an attorney?** Yes, for signing + state-specific formalities. The skill makes the attorneys job smaller, not optional. (Cross-reference section .18.)
3. **Is my data private?** The agent runs on your computer, not ours. The skill never transmits your financial documents anywhere — it reads local files and writes local files.
4. **What if Im not in the U.S.?** The skill is U.S.-only today. Foreign-asset overlays exist for U.S. domiciliaries with assets abroad, but if you are domiciled outside the U.S., this is not for you yet.
5. **Will the skill work if tax law changes?** The skill separates evergreen methodology from volatile law. Every live-law check is verified against primary sources and logged in `official-source-log.md`.
6. **Why $20/month instead of $20 once?** The skill is a living system — law updates, improvements, new subagents — that accrues value over time. Cancel anytime.
7. **What if I only need a simple will?** Youre probably better served by LegalZoom or Trust & Will. The skills value is in coordination across 8+ document types; if you just need one document, its overkill.
8. **How is this different from LegalZoom / Trust & Will / Rocket Lawyer?** (Link to the comparison section or paragraph — separate bead .38 if we build a full table.)
9. **Can I share the skill with my spouse / partner?** One subscription per user. Your spouse needs their own. The watermark on every bundle is tied to your user ID.
10. **What happens when I cancel?** You keep anything youve already produced — those are your files on your computer. You lose access to future updates + re-download. If you cancel mid-planning, you can finish offline with whats already on disk.
11. **Does it work on my phone?** Claude Code Desktop and Codex Desktop are Mac/Windows/Linux — no mobile app today. You need a computer.
12. **Can my attorney see what the skill produced?** Yes — the Attorney Engagement Brief is designed for exactly that. Take it to your lawyer; they will thank you.
13. **What if I get partway through and lose interest?** Your `intake-record.md` persists. Come back in a month, three months, or a year. The skill has a `maintenance-review` mode for periodic refresh.
14. **Is my crypto safe? Youre asking me about my seed phrase?** The skill NEVER asks for your seed phrase. The `digital-inventory.md` template has an explicit field that says "pointer to where your seed phrase is stored — do NOT put the seed phrase itself here."
15. **How long does this take?** A first full pass is typically 3-6 hours spread across a weekend, not a single sitting. Updates after life events take 30-60 min.

## Placement
Between the "Attorney handoff" section (.18) and the "TL;DR / pattern" section (.19). Add to the TOC anchor list.

## Accessibility
Each Q/A is a `<details>` element with `<summary>` for the question — native keyboard + screen-reader support, no JS needed.

## Acceptance criteria
- FAQ renders between .18 and .19.
- TOC includes the FAQ anchor.
- 15+ questions answered.
- Every Q has a plain-English A under 120 words.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:08:28.475969991Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:33:56.428453648Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:33:56.425616927Z","close_reason":"Code complete, verified by IvoryOrchid","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.37","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:08:28.475969991Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.37","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:53.443371209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.38","title":"Comparison paragraph/table vs LegalZoom, Trust & Will, Rocket Lawyer","description":"# Comparison vs. competitor services

The $20/month price makes a serious reader ask "why not LegalZoom for $79?" Directly addressing this is the difference between the reader subscribing and bouncing.

## Placement
Inside the "Who this is for" section (.12) or just after it, as a compact comparison block. NOT a massive table — readers glaze at multi-column tables. Prefer a 4-5 row compact table or a scannable bulleted contrast.

## Content — three axes that matter
| Dimension | LegalZoom / Trust & Will / Rocket Lawyer | wills-and-estate-planning-skill |
|-----------|------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|
| Scope | Produces one document (a will, a trust, a POA) from a form-based intake | Coordinates will + trust + beneficiary forms + titling + POAs + directives + digital inventory so every document tells one coherent story |
| Coordination check | None — the will is produced in isolation from your 401(k), deeds, insurance | Beneficiary audit + titling audit + coherence audit catch the 80% of real-world failures before they happen |
| State-law verification | Static templates (may lag by months after law changes) | Verification-first discipline — live-law lookups against primary sources, logged |
| Attorney handoff | The document is the endpoint; if you want an attorney, start over with them | Every session ends with an Attorney Engagement Brief designed to cut your first billable meeting from 8-15 hours to 2-3 |
| Cost at similar depth | $79-499 per document, or ongoing subscription at $150+/yr for bundle | $20/month, cancel anytime |

## Honest positioning
Be explicit: "If you just need a single will and you have uncomplicated finances, LegalZoom or Trust & Will are fine. The skill is for the case where the will is not the whole plan — a blended family, a business, a vacation home in a second state, a vulnerable heir, a crypto wallet, or enough net worth that state estate tax matters."

Do NOT disparage the competitors. They serve a real need and the reader may already use them. Position jeffreys-skills.md as "when thats not enough."

## Acceptance criteria
- Comparison block exists either inside .12 or as a standalone callout between .12 and .13.
- Honest framing ("LegalZoom is fine for X; the skill is for Y").
